name: Barbell Bench Press
form_cues: Shoulder blades retracted and pinned down through the entire set
Slight arch in upper back, feet planted, full-body tension
Bar touches lower chest (nipple line), elbows tucked ~45° from torso
Always use a spotter or safeties when going heavy
notes: Optional rotation with flat DB bench. Set safety pins to chest level. PF has multiple flat benches with rack uprights.
video_id_query: Barbell bench press proper form Jeff Nippard
instructions: Lie back on the bench with eyes directly under the bar. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Retract shoulder blades, plant feet, create full-body tension. Lift the bar off the rack and hold over the chest with locked elbows.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the lower chest (around the nipple line). Elbows ~45° from torso, not flared 90°.
Touch the chest lightly (no bounce), press the bar back up in a slight arc toward the face — finish over the shoulders.
Exhale at the top, keep shoulder blades retracted throughout.
Re-rack carefully when the set is done; bring it back to the J-hooks, don't drop it.
